facts: Lionel Messi became the first player in history to score in seven consecutive World Cup matches, extending his all-time record to 19 goals.
Harry Kane became England's all-time World Cup top scorer with 11 goals, surpassing Gary Lineker's record of 10.
Austria reached the knockout stage for the first time since 1982, surviving a 96th-minute Kalajdzic equaliser against Algeria in one of the most dramatic finishes in group-stage history.
Panama became the only team out of all 48 sides to leave the tournament without scoring a single goal.
Today's match is the first-ever Round of 32 game in tournament history — the opening match of the expanded knockout format.
Both South Africa and Canada are playing in a World Cup knockout match for the first time ever.
The two nations have met only once before — a friendly in 2007 — making this their first competitive encounter.
South Africa never advanced past the group stage before this tournament, including when they hosted in 2010.
Netherlands vs Morocco falls exactly 32 years to the day since the two sides met at USA '94 — also in a World Cup on American soil. Current Dutch coach Ronald Koeman started that match.
Brazil have won 11 of their 14 all-time meetings with Japan, but Japan stunned them 3-2 in a friendly in October 2025 — their first-ever win over the five-time champions.
Germany's 10 group-stage goals came from seven different scorers — the most balanced attack at this tournament. Deniz Undav scored three without starting a single match.
Morocco's 2022 semi-final run was the best finish by an African nation in World Cup history; the Netherlands are three-time finalists who have never won it.
